---
title: "Trust as an Entry Barrier: Evidence from FinTech Adoption"
author: "Yang, K."
date: "2025"
date-format: "YYYY"
description: "continuous-intensity DiD: Wells-Fargo-scandal exposure × post on FinTech adoption"
categories:
  - "DID_PLR"
  - "Finance (DiD)"
  - "2025"
  - "Extension demo"
image: forest_plot.png
paper-journal: "Journal of Financial Economics"
paper-doi: "10.1016/j.jfineco.2025.104062"
recast-path: "DID_PLR"
replication-regime: "stochastic"
---

## Summary

**Citation:** Yang, K. (2025). *Trust as an Entry Barrier: Evidence from FinTech Adoption*. *Journal of Financial Economics*. [doi:10.1016/j.jfineco.2025.104062](https://doi.org/10.1016/j.jfineco.2025.104062)

A continuous-treatment difference-in-differences. The estimand router correctly chose PLR on the two-way-FE-residualized panel (DID_PLR) — NOT the staggered DoubleMLDIDMulti, which is invalid for a continuous dose. The paper's effect (exposure × post → FinTech mortgage share) replicates (TWFE 0.019, t=3.5) and the DML extension confirms it: 0.020–0.022 across learners, all significant.

::: {.glance}
<div class="g"><span class="k">Method path</span><span class="v">DID_PLR</span></div>
<div class="g"><span class="k">Replication</span><span class="v">stochastic</span></div>
<div class="g"><span class="k">Review verdict</span><span class="v">Extension demo</span></div>
<div class="g"><span class="k">Identification</span><span class="v">DID</span></div>
:::

## Estimand & identification

{{< include estimand_statement.md >}}

## Replication

**Regime:** stochastic · **Gate:** PASS · **Overall tier:** PARTIAL

No deterministic published target is available, so replication is a documented *partial*. Our estimate: **0.0193** (SE 0.0056), n = 8194.

[No deterministic published target available (paper paywalled, .do code not in the data package); replication is a documented PARTIAL on the sign+significance of the two-way FE DiD.]{.text-muted-sm}

## The gap table

Original result, our replication/extension, the published benchmark (where one exists), and the verdict. The estimator never saw the benchmark — it is compared only after the results were frozen.


**Continuous-intensity DiD (FinTech share ~ exposure x post)**

| Estimator | Original | Ours | Benchmark | Verdict |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| TWFE DiD (replication) | not in package (paywalled) | 0.0193 (se 0.0056, t 3.46) | none (2025 paper; no revisit) | PARTIAL |

**DML extension (DID_PLR: two-way FE + PLR)**

| Estimator | Original | Ours | Benchmark | Verdict |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| DML - Lasso | - | 0.0207 (se 0.0053, p 0.000) | none | consistent |
| DML - Forest | - | 0.0203 (se 0.0053, p 0.000) | none | consistent |
| DML - Boosting | - | 0.0217 (se 0.0055, p 0.000) | none | consistent |
| DML - Trees | - | 0.0138 (se 0.0046, p 0.003) | none | consistent |

*Verdict counts:* PARTIAL 1, consistent 4.

::: {.callout-warning}
## Note
Continuous-treatment panel DiD. The DML extension (DID_PLR) absorbs county + year fixed effects with a two-way within transform and partials out covariates with ML, then a PLR on the exposure x post interaction. The headline effect survives and slightly strengthens under flexible nuisance controls. DoubleMLDIDMulti is NOT used (it requires binary treatment timing). Trust-mechanism (Gallup) tables are not replicable (pseudo data).
:::


## Causal-ML extension

![Continuous-intensity DiD: TWFE replication vs the DML (DID_PLR) extension across learners (95% CIs).](forest_plot.png)

Per-learner numbers are in the gap table above. 
All learners are the DML difference-in-differences estimator described above.

## What causal ML added

The cleanest DiD case on the site. Treatment is a continuous Wells-Fargo-exposure intensity interacted with the post-2016-scandal period — a 'dose' DiD, not binary staggered adoption — so the router sends it to DID_PLR: unit + time fixed effects absorbed by a two-way within transform, then DoubleML PLR on the interaction, clustered by county (the DML analogue of a two-way-FE DiD). On the 842-county panel the headline effect survives and slightly strengthens under flexible ML control (0.019 → 0.020–0.022), so the conclusion is robust, not an artifact of the linear control specification. The trust-mechanism (Gallup) tables use restricted pseudo data and are out of scope.

## AI peer review

This run is a **replication + causal-ML extension demonstration** of the difference-in-differences pathway; it has not yet been put through the two-referee AI review (unlike the studies above).

## Downloads & reproducibility

- Data: county panel range-extracted from the 1.4 GB Mendeley package (HMDA_FIPS.dta) without downloading the 4.3 GB loan-level file; built by the project's `build_project.py`.
- Full result artifacts (gap table, frozen estimates, referee reports) live in the project's `data/results/` and `paper/review_history/`.
